description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a dark, multi-faceted hip roof and a prominent front porch. The exterior walls are clad in light tan brick, contrasted by dark window frames and roof tiles. The front facade features a bay window section and a covered entryway supported by columns, with steps leading up to the main door. Windows are arranged in vertical pairs, with some grouped in threes, and the house has a single chimney protruding from the roof. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and minimal decorative elements. The overall design is solid and symmetrical, with a focus on functional, modern suburban aesthetics.
description: A two-story tan brick house with a dark hip roof, bay windows, and a covered front porch. The design is modern and symmetrical, with vertical window groupings and a prominent entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bay window, covered porch with steps, and main entry door.
description_view_1: Side view with a plain brick wall, minimal windows, and the chimney visible on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying symmetrical vertical window pairs and the full expanse of the hip roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a tall vertical window and a partial view of the bay window section.
attribute_window_arrangement: vertical strips and bay windows, mostly symmetrical
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, supported by columns, and a single main door beneath an overhang
